label_source: Layer-1 resolve pattern cmp,vp,vpc,naive = L,W,W,_ : cmplog BLOCKS, value_profile + value_profile_cmplog RESOLVE, naive non-decisive. The only technique delta between the involved winner (vp/vpc) and loser (cmplog) is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback (both carry the same I2S substitution mutator). The shape's Layer-1 attribution is therefore VP CMP_MAP gradient; this test is the INDEPENDENT real-data verification (G2) of HOW that gradient advantage manifests.
round0_refuted: Round-0 bet the whole shape on ONE story: VP's gradient closes scalar value-DISTANCE at the gate operand (measured via value_distance_reached -> vp_min_distance << cmp_min_distance). The deterministic arbiter scored that against real seeds and NONE held. The scalar-distance-closure mechanism is REFUTED for the seed-bearing branches and is NOT re-proposed.
round1_outcome: Round-1 pivoted to two corpus-state mechanisms. (1) vp_enriches_gate_operand_bytes (operand_enrichment) CONFIRMED the 3 harfbuzz codepoint gates (6025/6037/6048) -> now FROZEN. (2) vp_admits_token_richer_assembly (joint_necessity) held for >=1 branch (KEEP) but FAILED for sqlite3_14493 (rule_not_met). Still UNLABELED entering round-2: libpng_3833 (magnitude inequality), libxml2_6792/6800/7069_h0/7138_h1 (DTD scanner / content-tree assembly), sqlite3_14493 (dotted-reference assembly).

hypotheses.label: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient drives corpus admission toward structurally larger / more-token-rich seeds: the VPC-resolving corpus physically CONTAINS more of the gate's required structural tokens (and is larger) than the cmplog-blocking corpus, because the gradient rewards partial assembly that edge-only feedback discards. This is a STRUCTURAL-ASSEMBLY / token-availability advantage, NOT scalar value-distance.

measurement.tool_mode: analyze_pair(winner_fz=value_profile_cmplog, loser_fz=cmplog) -- LWW_ has NO plain value_profile blocking seeds, so the joint i2s_necessary/vp_tags arm is N/A; the discriminating contrast is the W=VPC-resolving vs L=cmplog-blocking pair the tool's analyze_pair mode already emits as winner_/loser_/tag_lift/size_lift.

tokens_by_target.sqlite3_14493: .,X.Y,X.Y.Z dotted-qualifier structure -- pass --tokens as the dotted-reference markers (literal '.' separators between identifiers; SQL keywords SELECT/FROM as structural anchors). The gate needs a 3-part schema-qualified name X.Y.Z -> count of '.'-joined identifier groups is the token signal.
tokens_by_target.harfbuzz_6025: the three required little-endian codepoint words 0x0930 (30 09 00 00), 0x094D (4d 09 00 00), 0x0907 (07 09 00 00) -- count of these adjacent codepoint tokens present.
tokens_by_target.harfbuzz_6048: the codepoint words 0x0A72 (72 0a 00 00) and the cur(1) members {0x0A3F,0x0A40,0x0A47} -- count of the matched-pair codepoint tokens present.
tokens_by_target.harfbuzz_6037: the single codepoint word 0x0A72 (72 0a 00 00) -- count present (single-token; weak token signal, leans on size_lift).

params.scope: ON-DISK CORPUS + SEEDS REQUIRED. DB-confirmed seed sets exist ONLY for harfbuzz_6025 (W=10/L=19), harfbuzz_6037 (W=6/L=10), harfbuzz_6048 (W=8/L=28), sqlite3_14493 (W=5/L=30). All four are W=value_profile_cmplog vs L=cmplog. The other 5 branches have ZERO seeds in db/blockers.sqlite on THIS server (libxml2/libpng are server-B targets) -- they are arbitrated by their owning server, not marked decidable:false here.

hypotheses.prediction: tag_lift = winner_tags - loser_tags >= 1.0 OR size_lift = winner_size/loser_size >= 1.3 : the VPC-resolving seed set carries at least one MORE of the gate's required structural tokens than the cmplog-blocking set, and/or is >=30% larger. This is DISCRIMINATING vs the refuted scalar-distance story AND vs a pure-I2S story: (1) it is independent of residual Hamming distance to any single operand -- it counts whole-token PRESENCE and assembly EXTENT, which round-0's distance metric did not; (2) cmplog carries the IDENTICAL I2S substitution mutator, so if the divergence were an I2S byte-substitution advantage the two seed sets would place tokens equally (tag_lift ~ 0, size_lift ~ 1.0). A positive tag_lift / size_lift is the signature uniquely attributable to VP's gradient-driven admission of more-assembled corpus members. ALTERNATIVE RULED OUT: 'cmplog already assembles the same structure, just stalls on a final scalar' -> would show tag_lift ~ 0 AND size_lift ~ 1.0.

hypotheses.label: secondary / corroborant: for the exact-codepoint conjunction gates, value_profile's gradient enriches the VPC corpus for the target operand bytes (vs decoy bytes) at the gate site, because near-miss codepoints register as new coverage and steer the corpus toward the exact operand.

params.scope: harfbuzz_6025 (0x0930/0x094D/0x0907), harfbuzz_6037 (0x0A72), harfbuzz_6048 (0x0A72 + {0x0A3F,0x0A40,0x0A47}) -- the contiguous/multibyte-literal codepoint gates. sqlite3_14493 EXCLUDED (its tokens are an assembled dotted STRUCTURE, not a fixed-offset byte literal -> byte_freq_ratio mis-applies).
params.caveat: operand_enrichment is anchored cmplog-vs-naive at a FIXED gate offset; here the codepoint operands sit at VARIABLE glyph indices (dispersed_multibyte for 6025/6048) and are short (2-byte 0x0A72 -> width<=2 chance-alignment risk flagged by the tool). Treat as CORROBORANT only; it does not stand alone as the verdict.

hypotheses.prediction: signed_target_enrich > 0 (and > decoy_enrich): the VPC-resolving corpus is enriched for the target codepoint bytes relative to decoy bytes at the gate site. Discriminating: a no-VP-advantage (pure-I2S) corpus would show signed_target_enrich ~ 0 (I2S substitutes the operand on demand without persistently enriching the saved corpus for near-misses). RULED OUT: chance co-occurrence -> would not exceed decoy_enrich.

hypotheses.label: NEW (round-2): for the STATE-MACHINE / RECURSIVE-ASSEMBLY gates, value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient admits corpus members that DYNAMICALLY REACH a deeper point in the parse/recursion than the cmplog-blocking corpus -- the winner seeds drive the gate line's execution count up and actually TAKE the decisive side, while the loser seeds stall BEFORE the gate line. The signature is per-seed REACH DEPTH (a runtime llvm-cov count), which is orthogonal to both the refuted scalar value-distance (round-0) and the static seed-size/token-count (vp_admits_token_richer_assembly, which FAILED for sqlite3_14493). This is the assembly-DEPTH manifestation: the parser is steered into state==']'/neutral (libxml2_6792/6800), builds a multi-child content tree yielding a non-NULL c2 (libxml2_7069_h0), climbs the multi-clause docname cascade into the true branch (libxml2_7138_h1), or assembles the X.Y.Z dotted reference so zDb is non-NULL at lookupName's error block (sqlite3_14493).

measurement.tool_mode: branch --winner-fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --loser-fuzzer cmplog : runs W=VPC-resolving seeds and L=cmplog-blocking seeds through the libafl-<target>-cov image, reads the gate line's per-seed-normalized execution count + whether the blocked side was taken. The vp-structural depth use the tool documents.

params.scope: Docker per-branch (~30-60s) on the OWNING server's cov image. sqlite3_14493 is seed-bearing + cov-image-present on s4 (W=5/L=30, libafl-sqlite3-cov exists). libxml2_6792/6800/7069_h0/7138_h1 are server-B targets (libafl-libxml2-cov exists; their seeds live in server-B's db) -> arbitrated by server B; DESIGNED to be valid there, NOT marked decidable:false merely for s4 corpus absence.

hypotheses.prediction: depth_lift = winner_line_count / loser_line_count >= 2.0 AND (winner_branch_taken AND NOT loser_branch_taken) : the VPC-resolving corpus reaches the gate line at least 2x more per seed AND takes the decisive (blocked) side, while the cmplog-blocking corpus stalls before the gate. This is DISCRIMINATING on THREE counts: (1) vs the REFUTED scalar value-distance -- depth is a runtime reach count at the gate line, not a residual Hamming distance to an operand, so a branch that failed value_distance_reached can still show depth_lift>>1 (the cards confirm dynamic reach: libxml2 'W shows T=4 at L11261 / L-only never reaches 11262-11286'; sqlite3 'd=1 winner blocker if(zDb) T=1 vs loser T=0 F=20'); (2) vs the FAILED static token-count -- depth_reach measures what the corpus EXECUTION reaches, not how big/token-rich the saved seed file is, so a branch (sqlite3_14493) whose seeds are not larger/token-richer can still drive the gate line deeper; (3) vs a pure-I2S story -- cmplog carries the identical I2S mutator, so if I2S byte-substitution alone solved the assembly both arms would reach the gate line equally (depth_lift ~ 1, both branch_taken). ALTERNATIVE RULED OUT: 'the loser corpus reaches the gate line just as deep and only takes the other side by value chance' -> would show depth_lift ~ 1 (NOT >=2) and loser_branch_taken in some seeds. G2-INDEPENDENT: depth_reach reads per-seed llvm-cov execution depth, a distinct signal from the aggregate final-checkpoint resolve/block trial outcome that DEFINED the shape.

hypotheses.label: honest non-discriminable (G3): libpng_3833 is a SINGLE scalar magnitude-inequality guard (greenx > PNG_FP_1 == 100000 == 0x000186A0). Its only mechanistically-fitting discriminator is scalar value-DISTANCE (value_distance_reached --numeric magnitude mode) -- and that mechanism was already SCORED for this branch and did NOT hold (round-1 reports libpng_3833 as 1/2 rules scored, none held). No OTHER built tool can discriminate a single magnitude-inequality gate: depth_reach mis-applies (greenx>... is one guard reached once per call, not a recursive/iterated assembly -> both arms reach png_XYZ_from_xy with depth_lift ~ 1; the difference is the VALUE, which a line-execution count cannot see); operand_enrichment needs a FIXED-OFFSET input-byte literal, but greenx is a DERIVED fixed-point value, not a verbatim byte literal in the input -> no_gate_signature; joint_necessity token-count has no structural token for a bare magnitude threshold. Re-proposing value_distance_reached would be a forbidden relaxed re-test of an already-refuted mechanism.
hypotheses.prediction: N/A -- the only fitting discriminator (scalar magnitude distance) is already refuted for this branch, and every OTHER built tool mis-applies to a single magnitude-inequality guard. There is no NEW built measurement that can independently confirm VP's mechanism here.
hypotheses.rule: decidable:false -> libpng_3833 carries the Layer-1 VP-gradient label but gets NO independent confirmation; the arbiter must leave it inconclusive (fallback). An honest non-discriminable is a CORRECT G3 outcome -- a mechanism is NOT invented to force a label.

data_realities_respected: value_profile (CMP_MAP) is a FEEDBACK not a mutator -> NOT measured via any seed_lineage 'gradient event'. Its effect is read off the corpus the gradient ADMITTED -- structural size+token count (joint_necessity), operand byte enrichment (operand_enrichment), and what the corpus EXECUTION REACHES (depth_reach) -- all corpus-state / corpus-reach computes, no lineage event.
I2S is NOT tagged in mutation_op -> NO seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence on an I2S event proposed.

on-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl,harfbuzz,openthread,sqlite3 on s4 (libxml2,libpng,lcms,bloaty on server B) -> CROSS-SERVER: the 4 libxml2 branches + libpng are NOT marked decidable:false for s4 corpus absence; they are arbitrated by their owning server (libafl-libxml2-cov exists for depth_reach; libpng_3833 is decidable:false on its OWN mechanistic grounds, not corpus availability).

LWW_ has NO plain value_profile arm in the corpus (value_profile RESOLVES, so no value_profile BLOCKING seeds) -> joint_necessity is used in analyze_pair(VPC vs cmplog) mode, and depth_reach in branch(winner=VPC, loser=cmplog) mode, NOT any 3-arm mode requiring a plain-vp blocking arm.
depth_reach reads per-seed llvm-cov EXECUTION depth at the gate line, distinct from the aggregate final-checkpoint resolve/block outcome that defines the shape -> G2-independent.

the structural-assembly / dotted-structure operands are NOT fixed-offset byte literals, so byte_freq_ratio (operand_enrichment) is applied ONLY to the contiguous codepoint gates (6025/6037/6048), never to sqlite3's assembled X.Y.Z structure or the libxml2 state-machine gates.

a single magnitude-inequality guard (greenx>100000) has no structural token, no fixed-offset input-byte literal, and is reached once per call -> joint_necessity / operand_enrichment / depth_reach all mis-apply; only value_distance_reached fits, and it is refuted for that branch.
notes:
PRIORS KEPT (superset / monotonic): both KEEP priors are carried forward VERBATIM so the confirmed branches re-validate. (1) vp_admits_token_richer_assembly (joint_necessity, analyze_pair) -- kept; confirmed >=1 branch in round-1. (2) vp_enriches_gate_operand_bytes (operand_enrichment) -- kept; this is the rule that CONFIRMED the 3 FROZEN harfbuzz branches (6025/6037/6048); decision_order keeps it so they re-validate.

NEW MECHANISM (round-2): vp_drives_corpus_deeper_into_assembly via the BUILT depth_reach tool (descriptor seed_set:depth_reach:branch_line:per_branch -- NO new descriptor needed, reuse by key). This is GENUINELY DIFFERENT from every mechanism already refuted for these branches: (a) NOT scalar value-distance (round-0, refuted) -- depth is a runtime reach count, not a Hamming residual; (b) NOT static seed-size/token-count (vp_admits_token_richer_assembly, which FAILED for sqlite3_14493) -- depth measures what the corpus EXECUTION reaches, not how big the saved seed is. Every one of the 5 member cards describes ASSEMBLY-DEPTH reach (steer the DTD scanner to state==']'/neutral; build a multi-child content tree -> non-NULL c2; climb the docname cascade; assemble X.Y.Z -> non-NULL zDb at the error block) with explicit llvm-cov evidence the WINNER reaches the gate line and the LOSER stalls before it -- exactly depth_reach's signal. This RESCUES sqlite3_14493 (seed-bearing + cov-image present on s4, so scorable HERE) and gives the 4 libxml2 branches a discriminable test on server B (libafl-libxml2-cov exists). depth_reach is documented as a 'vp-structural' use and is G2-independent (reads per-seed execution depth, not the resolve label) -- Round-1's dismissal of it (' also seed-driven, not G2-independent') was wrong on both counts: G2 turns on signal independence (execution depth != resolve label), not on whether seeds are read; and the no-seeds objection only applied to s4, where these targets' corpora do not live.

DECIDABLE:FALSE (honest G3): libpng_3833 ONLY. It is a single scalar magnitude-inequality guard (greenx>100000). Its only fitting discriminator -- scalar value-distance (value_distance_reached --numeric) -- was already scored for it and refuted (round-1: 1/2 rules scored, none held), and re-proposing it is a forbidden relaxed re-test. No OTHER built tool discriminates a bare magnitude gate: depth_reach -> depth_lift~1 (one guard, reached once, value invisible to a line count); operand_enrichment -> no fixed-offset byte literal (greenx is a derived value); joint_necessity -> no structural token. So libpng_3833 is HONESTLY non-discriminable and falls to inconclusive. I do NOT invent a mechanism to force a label.

CROSS-SERVER: rules designed to be valid for ALL branches regardless of which server holds the corpus. The 4 libxml2 branches are seedless on s4 only because libxml2 is a server-B target; they are NOT marked decidable:false for that reason -- the owning server arbitrates them with depth_reach. The only decidable:false branch (libpng_3833) is so on intrinsic-mechanism grounds, not corpus availability.

NO NEW DESCRIPTOR NEEDED: all three decidable hypotheses reuse a BUILT registry tool by descriptor_key (joint_necessity = seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count:tokens:per_branch; operand_enrichment = corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio:target_vs_decoy:per_gate_offset; depth_reach = seed_set:depth_reach:branch_line:per_branch). The only adaptations are existing entry-point modes (analyze_pair / branch winner-vs-loser) the tools already expose -- no code change.

EXPECTED OUTCOME: the 3 harfbuzz branches stay CONFIRMED (frozen). sqlite3_14493 -- the strongest assembly-depth case (TK_DOT dispatch -> zDb non-null at lookupName) -- most likely confirms under depth_reach where it failed token-count. The 4 libxml2 branches are scorable on server B; if a libxml2 branch shows depth_lift~1 there it correctly falls to inconclusive (a clean G3 negative, not chased to 100%). libpng_3833 stays inconclusive (decidable:false).
